title: Molten Mustardy Dutch Potato Fritters
description: Show-off Dutch dish featuring russet potato, katsuramuki-cut and a surprising molten mustard filling.
instructions: Peel the russet potatoes and cut them into 1/4-inch thick slices.
Cut each slice into katsuramuki-cut sticks.
In a large bowl, combine soy flour, mustard paste, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add water to the bowl and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350°F (175°C).
Add a portion of the potato sticks to the hot oil and fry until golden brown.
Drain the fried potatoes on paper towels and set aside.
To assemble the fritters, place a portion of the fried potatoes on a plate.
Drizzle the mustard paste mixture in the center of the potatoes.
Place another portion of the fried potatoes on top of the mustard paste.
Finish with a drizzle of sesame oil and a sprinkle of sesame seeds.
